title: Quetzalia standleyi
intro: Quetzalia standleyi, also known as Mexican Quetzalia, is a medicinal plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Quetzalia standleyi include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and relieve respiratory discomfort.
taxonomy: Quetzalia standleyi is classified within the plant family Calophyllaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. This plant family is part of the order Malpighiales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. The Malpighiales order is part of the clade Eurosidae II, which is a clade of flowering plants. The Eurosidae II clade is part of the larger clade Rosids, which is one of the two main groups of flowering plants. This classification is based on molecular and morphological studies of the plant.
